text.title: Token File Formats and Organisation
text.summary: JSON token definition format for each category (color, spacing, typography, radius, shadow) and directory organisation convention.

section.title: Token File Format

rule.statement: Define each token as a nested JSON object with a value key. Use {category.scale} reference syntax for aliases. Group files by layer: primitive.json, semantic.json, semantic-dark.json, components/.
rule.rationale: Style Dictionary and Tokens Studio both consume this W3C community group format. Consistent structure enables automated multi-platform compilation.

section.reference: Full primitive token file: colors, spacing, fontSize, fontWeight, borderRadius, shadow
Semantic light theme: bg, text, border, action, status, focus ring aliases

rule.statement: Cover at minimum: color (full scale), spacing (0–20), fontSize (xs–4xl), fontWeight (normal/medium/semibold/bold), borderRadius (none–full), shadow (sm–xl). Add animation/duration tokens when motion is part of the design system.
rule.rationale: Incomplete token coverage forces developers to hardcode values in components, re-introducing the single-source-of-truth problem the token system was meant to solve.

example.note: Primitive spacing scale using rem — device-independent and accessible.

rule.statement: Store token files under tokens/ at the project root, mirroring the three-level hierarchy. Component token files go in tokens/components/ named after the component.
rule.rationale: A predictable directory structure allows tooling (Style Dictionary transforms) to be configured once and applied to all tokens without per-file overrides.

antipattern.description: Flat tokens/ directory with all tokens in a single file.
antipattern.reason: A single file conflates primitive and semantic levels, making it impossible to swap theme files without touching the full token set.
